data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-93d2ac52">
    <title>execute-bead: harness child orphans to PID 1 when ddx work parent dies abnormally (SIGKILL/crash) — no Pdeathsig, no startup reaper</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
When a `ddx work` orchestrator dies abnormally (SIGKILL, crash, OOM, host reboot of the supervising process) while a bead attempt is in flight, the spawned harness child (e.g. `claude --print -p --verbose --output-format stream-json ... <execute-bead>` or `codex exec --json ...`) is NOT reaped. It reparents to PID 1 and keeps running indefinitely. Observed 2026-05-27 on the dev host: a `claude --print` execute-bead child orphaned with PPID 1 for ~9h53m after its `ddx work` parent had gone — burning a harness session and (per the sibling fizeau leak) anchoring leaked subprocess trees. This is the orchestrator-side mirror of fizeau-50b6f055 (harness-runner-side leak).

ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 — OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir spawns the harness via exec.Command(binary, args...) then cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd).
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 — cmdSetProcessGroup sets ONLY SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}. There is NO Pdeathsig. So the kernel does nothing automatically when the parent dies.
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:20-25 — cmdKillProcessGroup(cmd) SIGKILLs the negative pgid, but it is only invoked from the executor's graceful paths (stopProcess at executor.go:190-194, triggered by idle/wall-clock timeout, auth/rate-limit pattern, or ctx cancellation at executor.go:231-255).
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 — the worker context is a signal.NotifyContext on os.Interrupt + SIGTERM only. Graceful shutdown works; SIGKILL and crashes cannot be caught, so the defer/stopProcess chain never runs and the child survives.
- Net: child harness lifetime is governed entirely by parent-run cleanup. If the parent does not get to run cleanup, the child orphans. There is no kernel-enforced parent-death signal and no startup reaper that adopts/kills harness children whose parent PID is gone.

SECONDARY VECTOR
- cli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:293 — the docker-clone backend spawns `exec.CommandContext(ctx, "docker", ...)` WITHOUT cmdSetProcessGroup, so even graceful cleanup may not reach the nested harness inside the container. (worktree/local-clone backends at attempt_backend.go:120-122 / :182-184 share the executor.go:156 path and do get Setpgid.)

PROPOSED FIX
1. Primary (Linux): set SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig = SIGKILL (or SIGTERM) in cmdSetProcessGroup so the kernel signals the harness child when the parent thread dies, regardless of how the parent died. Note the well-known caveat: Pdeathsig keys off the spawning OS *thread*, so pin/guard against Go runtime thread migration (runtime.LockOSThread around Start, or re-check parent PID in the child path). Document the macOS/Windows gap (Pdeathsig is Linux-only) and the chosen fallback there.
2. Backstop: on `ddx work` startup, reap orphaned harness children from prior dead workers — e.g. when registering/claiming, scan for execute-bead harness processes whose parent is PID 1 (or whose recorded owner PID is gone) and SIGKILL their process groups, gated to this project/workspace to avoid nuking unrelated work. Ties to the existing lease-release / operator-attention machinery (cross-ref ddx-8f2e0ebf).
3. docker-clone backend: apply cmdSetProcessGroup to the docker invocation at attempt_backend.go:293 (and/or run with --rm + a container-stop on cleanup) so the container tree is reaped too.

NON-SCOPE
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works (executor.go:231-255); do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this bead is the ddx orchestrator -> harness-child boundary.

CROSS-REF: fizeau-50b6f055 (fizeau harness-runner process-group cleanup), ddx-8f2e0ebf (lease release / operator-attention).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. A harness child spawned by the execute-bead executor does NOT survive abnormal death of its parent worker. Verified by a test that starts the executor against a fake harness binary which forks a long-lived grandchild, then SIGKILLs the parent worker process (not graceful SIGTERM) and asserts the harness child + grandchild are gone (not PID-1 orphans) within a bounded grace. On Linux this is satisfied via SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig in cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go.
2. cmdSetProcessGroup sets Pdeathsig (Linux) and the thread-migration caveat is handled (LockOSThread or equivalent) so the death signal actually fires; a test or documented rationale covers the non-Linux fallback.
3. `ddx work` startup reaps orphaned execute-bead harness children from prior dead workers scoped to the current project/workspace, and does NOT kill harness children of other live workers. Verified by a test that plants a fake orphaned harness process (parent gone) and a fake live-owned one and asserts only the orphan is reaped.
4. The docker-clone backend (attempt_backend.go:293) configures process-group/container cleanup so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit; covered by a test or, if container teardown already guarantees this, a documented justification.
5. Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown still terminates in-flight harness children (regression guard).
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, priority:reliability, severity:high, observability</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="8c31bfc98651c609153e5f91e296f5e4ca966d0b" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260603T190608-0c6908c8"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All work for `ddx-93d2ac52` was already completed by prior child beads. No code change is needed.

**Outcome: `no_changes` with verification command.**

Every AC is provably satisfied by existing code + tests, and the agent suite passes green (108s):

| AC | Code | Test |
|----|------|------|
| 1 — Pdeathsig kills child on abnormal parent death | `executor_unix.go:28` | `TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_LinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessOnParentSIGKILL` |
| 2 — thread-migration guard + non-Linux fallback | `executor.go:183-185` | `…ParentDeathSignalThreadGuard`, `…NonLinuxParentDeathFallbackDocumentsGap` |
| 3 — startup reaper, project-scoped, spares live children | `execute_bead_loop.go:1246` | `TestWorkStartup_Reaps…`, `…DoesNotReapLiveOwnedChildren`, `…ReaperDoesNotKillOtherWorkspaceHarness` |
| 4 — docker-clone process-group + `--rm --init` | `attempt_backend.go:799,727` | `TestDockerCloneBackend_*` |
| 5 — graceful shutdown regression guard | (existing cancel path) | `…GracefulCancellationStillKillsProcessGroup` |
| 6 — suite green | — | ✓ |

I wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bundle path (`.ddx/executions/20260603T190608-0c6908c8/`) with `verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...`, which exits 0 and closes the bead. No commit was made since there are no source changes; returning control to the orchestrator.
